---
title: Rust quick start
description: Build an ACT component in Rust with the act-sdk macros.
---

## Scaffold

```bash
copier copy gh:actcore/act-template-rust my-component
cd my-component
just init
```

The template sets up `Cargo.toml` with `act-sdk = "0.5"`, a `wit/` directory tracking [`act:core@0.3.0`](https://github.com/actcore/act-spec/blob/main/wit/act-core.wit), a `rust-toolchain.toml` pinning nightly + `wasm32-wasip2`, and a `justfile` with `build` / `test` / `publish` recipes.

## Write a tool

`src/lib.rs`:

```rust
use act_sdk::prelude::*;

act_sdk::embed_skill!("skill/");

#[act_component]
mod component {
    use super::*;

    /// Reverse a string.
    #[act_tool(description = "Reverse a string", read_only)]
    fn reverse(
        #[doc = "Text to reverse"] text: String,
    ) -> ActResult<String> {
        Ok(text.chars().rev().collect())
    }
}
```

The `#[act_component]` macro generates the WIT `tool-provider` export. The `#[act_tool]` macro derives the JSON Schema from the function signature, collects `std:*` metadata (`read_only`, `idempotent`, `destructive`, `timeout_ms`), and registers the function with the component's dispatcher.

## Build

```bash
just build
```

This runs:
1. `cargo build --release` â†’ `target/wasm32-wasip2/release/<name>.wasm`
2. `act-build pack <name>.wasm` â†’ embeds the `act:component` custom section (CBOR-encoded metadata) and the contents of `skill/` as an Agent Skill tar.

## Verify

```bash
act info target/wasm32-wasip2/release/component_my_component.wasm --tools
```

Should print your component name, version, and the `reverse` tool with its auto-derived schema.

## Run

```bash
just test                    # e2e hurl tests against the built component
act call <wasm> reverse --args '{"text":"hello"}'
```

## What `act-sdk` gives you

| Macro / type | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `#[act_component]` | Wraps a module as the `act:core/tool-provider` export |
| `#[act_tool(â€¦)]` | Registers a fn as a tool, emits JSON Schema from the signature + doc comments |
| `ActError` / `ActResult` | Return errors with a `std:*` kind and localized message |
| `Json<T>` | Opt into JSON encoding (default is CBOR, per [ACT-SPEC](https://github.com/actcore/act-spec/blob/main/spec/ACT-SPEC.md)) |
| `Content { mime, data }` | Return raw bytes with an explicit MIME type |
| `embed_skill!("skill/")` | Compile-time embed of `skill/SKILL.md` + friends |
